generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot, controlled via Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl platform with separate axis control for position, speed, or acceleration regulation"
]

instance GearboxMechanism : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Gearbox mechanism limiting motion range and introducing mechanical limitations for the first axis actuation system"
]

instance ControlSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl platform implementing closed-loop feedback mechanisms for safety-constrained actuation with implicit operational limit compliance"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the motor for high-resolution position feedback of the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
]

instance SecondaryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the gearbox providing secondary high-resolution position feedback for the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
]

instance MotorTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor integrated with the motor to monitor operational temperature during machining applications"
    DTDFVocab:producedData StateData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder, SecondaryPositionEncoder
    base:desc "High-resolution position data streams transmitted via MQTT for real-time tracking of motor and mechanical states"
]

instance MotorStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder, MotorTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Aggregated state data including position and temperature transmitted via MQTT for operational monitoring"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance GeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD-based geometric representation of the first axis mechanical structure including motor and gearbox components for spatial analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnvironment
]

instance BehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "State machine model for operational workflows of the first axis, implemented in SysML v1.7 for behavior validation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnvironment
]

instance PhysicsBasedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation model for performance and reliability assessment of the first axis mechanical system"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnvironment
]

instance DigitalShadowModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Contextual data trace aggregation model for passive digital shadow representation of the first axis system"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationSystem
]

instance MotionAccuracyModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Friction modeling and motion accuracy improvement model specifically designed for machining applications"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlOptimizationService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MBSEFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Based Systems Engineering framework implementing RFLP methodology for structured abstraction hierarchies across requirements, functional, logical, and technical layers"
]

instance SysMLModeling : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML implementation for requirements, functional/logical architecture, and behavior modeling (state machines) of the first axis system"
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralModel, DigitalShadowModel
]

instance AADLArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Architecture Description Language (AADL) implementation for component, interface, and connector definitions in the first axis system"
    DTDFVocab:enables GeometricModel, PhysicsBasedModel
]

instance SimulationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for detailed behavior modeling, validation, and integration of first axis system models"
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralModel, PhysicsBasedModel
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message Queuing Telemetry Transport protocol for real-time data transmission between sensors and Digital Twin system"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotorPositionData, MotorStateData
]

instance DataAggregationSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "System for contextual data trace aggregation and abstraction for digital shadow representation"
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalShadowModel
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software system for predictive and adaptive control integration using motion accuracy models"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionAccuracyModel
]

// C12: Data
instance PositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "High-resolution position data streams from encoders for real-time tracking of motor and mechanical states"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowModel, ControlOptimizationService
]

instance StateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated state data including position and temperature for operational monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowModel, MotionAccuracyModel
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature readings from motor sensor for operational reliability assessment"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorTemperatureSensor
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PhysicsBasedModel
]

// C6: Services
instance OperationalMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time operational monitoring of the first axis system through aggregated sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides InsightPositionAccuracy, InsightTemperatureTrends
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing predictive and adaptive control algorithms for motion accuracy improvement"
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionMotionCorrection, InsightFrictionModel
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for visual representation of system states and performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:provides InsightStateVisualization
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance InsightPositionAccuracy : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time insight into position tracking accuracy of the first axis motor system"
]

instance InsightTemperatureTrends : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Operational temperature trend analysis for motor reliability assessment"
]

instance InsightFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Friction modeling insights for motion accuracy improvement in machining applications"
]

instance InsightStateVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of system operational states and performance metrics"
]

instance ActionMotionCorrection : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Autonomous motion correction action triggered by position accuracy deviations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ActionTemperatureAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Autonomous temperature threshold alert action for operational safety monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnvironmentFirstAxis : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearboxMechanism, ControlSystem,
              MotorPositionEncoder, SecondaryPositionEncoder, MotorTemperatureSensor,
              DigitalShadowModel, MotionAccuracyModel
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The provided documents do not explicitly detail synchronization frequencies, latency requirements, or temporal granularity for Digital Twin (DT) systems. However, the distinction between **Digital Shadows** and **Digital Twins** implies that while a Digital Shadow operates as a **passive, one-way data flow** (e.g., aggregated contextual data traces without feedback), a DT requires **active components** to interpret and process data in real-time or near-real-time. This suggests that **twinning_time_scale** in a DT context would involve **time-sensitive synchronization mechanisms** (e.g., aligned with physical system operational cycles, such as in manufacturing processes), though no specific frequencies (e.g., milliseconds, seconds) or tools (e.g., time synchronization protocols like NTP) are mentioned. The absence of explicit documentation implies that temporal aspects are implicitly tied to the **communication flow** between physical and digital entities, where **latency** would depend on the underlying data aggregation and processing infrastructure (e.g., cloud-based systems, edge computing)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** is not explicitly defined in the documents, but the **Digital Shadow reference model** (Fig. 1) outlines a foundational concept where a DT could serve as an **active component** to enable bidirectional interaction with physical systems. The **orchestration and coordination** of a DT system would likely rely on:
   - **Architecture patterns** derived from ADLs (e.g., MontiArc’s component-connector model, SysML’s behavior descriptions).
   - **Resource management** through integration with simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation frameworks) to model behavior in detail.
   - **System-wide coordination** via **model-based systems engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) that handle complexity across multiple abstraction layers (requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, product/technical layers).
   The documents imply a **multi-layered, tool-agnostic approach** where DTs are embedded within a broader **MBSE framework**, but no specific constellation patterns (e.g., federated twins, hybrid cloud architectures) or integration protocols are detailed. Thus, this characteristic is **not in Document** for a DT system’s orchestration, though it may be inferred from broader MBSE principles."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity is ensured through the comparison of **simulated data** (e.g., friction analysis models) with **measured data** from physical sensors. The Digital Twin’s validity is grounded in the **digital shadow’s passive aggregation** of contextual data traces, where deviations between simulation and measurement are implicitly used for validation. No explicit uncertainty quantification, error detection mechanisms, or quality assurance processes are detailed, though the comparison framework implies iterative refinement of models to align with real-world behavior."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The provided documents emphasize **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** as a foundational approach for Digital Twin (DT) development, leveraging **systems engineering modeling languages** such as **SysML** for structured representation. Key methodologies for handling complexity include:
- **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** for hierarchical decomposition,
- **OOSEM (Object-Oriented Systems Engineering Methodology)**,
- **SPES (Systematic Process Engineering for Systems)**,
- **SMArDT (SysML-based Architecture for Digital Twins)**.

**Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, MontiArc**, and **SysML IBDs** are used to model components, connectors, interfaces, and interconnections, with some ADLs (e.g., MontiArc Automaton, SysML state machines) enabling behavioral modeling. These models are often integrated with simulation tools (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink**) or general-purpose languages (e.g., **C++, Java**) for dynamic analysis.

While the documents do not explicitly reference **standardization frameworks** (e.g., ISO/IEC 15288 for lifecycle management), the reliance on **SysML, AADL, and MBSE methodologies** implies adherence to industry-standard practices for **systems engineering documentation and interoperability**. The described approaches ensure **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **multi-tool compatibility**, though no explicit compliance to specific DT-centric standards (e.g., ISO/IEC 4293:2019) is documented."
]
